PROCLAMATION
TO ALL TO WHOM THESE PRESENTS SHALL COME :
WHEREAS , the present structure of the state and local govern-
ment of Kentucky obstructs the economic and ef-
fective management of state and local business ; and
WHEREAS , bringing together the multiplicity of scattered
boards , commissions , departments and other agen-
cies of the state and local government into a sys-
tematic and orderly plan is essential to effective
administration at minimum expense ; and
WHEREAS , preparation of a budget to provide for the economi-
cal financing of state and local services is depen-
dent on prior reorganization of the state and local
government ;
NOW , THEREFORE , in order to insure the people of the
Commonwealth a reorganized and efficient plan
of government , and in accordance with the power
vested in me by Section 80 of the Constitution of
Kentucky to convene the General Assembly into
Extraordinary Session, as Governor of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , I, A. B. Chandler , do issue
this , my proclamation , convening the General As-
sembly of Kentucky in Extraordinary Session at the
seat of government at Frankfort, Kentucky , at 7
p.m. , CST on Monday, February 27, 1956 , for the
sole purpose of considering the following subject :
( 1) To pass acts reorganizing and simplify-
ing the state and local government .
You will therefore , in accordance with this proclamation ,
assemble in Extraordinary Session at the seat of government
at Frankfort , Kentucky , on the day and hour above written , to
consider the subject herein mentioned , and only said subject .
Done at Frankfort, Kentucky , this the 23rd
day of February in the year of our Lord , one
thousand nine hundred and fifty six, and in the
year of the Commonwealth the one hundred and
sixty- fourth .
( Signed ) A. B. CHANDLER , Governor
( Signed ) Thelma L. Stovall
Secretary of State

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ,
KENTUCKY, ON MONDAY , FEBRUARY THE TWENTY - SEVENTH ,
AND ENDED ON THURSDAY , MARCH THE EIGHTH , 1956
CHAPTER 1
(S.B. 1 )
AN ACT to amend the Southern Regional Education Compact to in-
crease the number of members of the Board of Control for Southern
Regional Education from four from each state to five from each
state and to specify that one member from each state shall be
appointed by the Governor from among the membership of the
legislature of that state .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That part of paragraph 2 of the Southern Re-
gional Education Compact which reads :
"The states do further hereby establish and create a joint
agency which shall be known as the Board of Control for
Southern Regional Education ( hereinafter referred to as the
"board " ) , the members of which board shall consist of the
Governor of each state , ex officio , and three additional citizens
of each state to be appointed by the Governor thereof , at least
one of whom shall be selected from the field of education ."
Shall be amended to read as follows :
"The states do further hereby establish and created a joint
agency which shall be known as the Board of Control for South-
ern Regional Education (hereinafter referred to as the "board "),
the members of which board shall consist of the Governor of
each state , ex officio , and four additional citizens of each state to
be appointed by the Governor thereof , at least one of whom
shall be selected from the field of education , and at least one
of whom shall be a member of the legislature of that state . "
Section 2. This amendment will take effect when eight or
more of the states party to the compact have given legislative
approval to the amendment .
Approved March 12 , 1956

8 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
CHAPTER 2
(S.B. 2)
AN ACT relating to circuit courts .
WHEREAS , the General Assembly has given due regard to the ele-
ments of territory , business and population involved in the division
of the state into judicial districts , and,
WHEREAS , with such regard , the General Assembly has found the
following facts to exist :
(1 ) The entire population of the state as of the last federal census
is 2,944,806 .
(2) That upon the basis of population , additional judicial districts
may be established to the forty -five judicial districts now existing ,
pursuant to Sections 132 and 138 of the Constitution .
(3) That the counties of Grayson , Breckinridge and Meade have a
combined population of 42,013 as of the last federal census .
(4) That the population of Hardin county was 50,312 as of the
last federal census and its population is rapidly increasing .
(5) That currently and heretofore the amount of business in the
Hardin circuit court is heavy and there is a considerable backlog
of cases pending before said court at this time and the business of
the Hardin circuit court combined with the business of the Grayson ,
Breckinridge and Meade circuit courts makes it difficult to prop-
erly take care of the business of all these circuit courts , the com-
bined business of the Hardin , Grayson , Breckinridge and Meade
circuit courts as the Ninth Judicial District as now existing is too
much , and these counties should be re-arranged and re -grouped .
(6 ) That the counties of Grayson , Meade and Breckinridge join
each other .
(7) That Hardin county has a large volume of cases from the
Fort Knox Military Reservation .
(8) That it will be convenient and advantageous from the stand-
point of business , territory and population and will comply with
the spirit and intent of the Constitution and expedite the business
of the courts , and make it more convenient to the citizens of the
counties involved , to create a new judicial district for the counties
of Grayson , Breckinridge and Meade , and to change the Ninth
Judicial District so that it will be composed of Hardin county .
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 23.040 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
The state is divided into forty-six circuit court districts ,
each to be composed of the following counties :

Section 2. Subsections ( 9 ) ( 35 ) and ( 46) of section 23.050
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are amended to read as follows :
The regular term of each court shall commence in each
county at the following named time , and continue , if the busi-
ness requires it, the number of days hereinafter prescribed :
( 9 ) Ninth District . Hardin county , at Elizabethtown , con-
tinuous session .
( 35 ) Thirty -fifth District . Pike county , at Pikeville , con-
tinuous session .
( 46 ) Forthy - sixth District . Breckinridge county at Hardins-
burg, on the first Monday in January for thirty juridical days ;
second Monday in May for twenty juridical days ; first Monday
in September for thirty juridical days .
Grayson county at Leitchfield, on the second Monday in
February for thirty juridical days ; on the second Monday in June
for twenty juridical days ; on the first Monday in October for
thirty juridical days .
Meade county at Brandenburg , on the third Monday in
March for thirty juridical days ; second Monday in July for twenty

CHAPTER 3 11
juridical days ; and first Monday in November for thirty juridical
days .
Section 3. The present circuit judge and Commonwealth's
attorney for the Ninth District as now constituted , shall retain
their respective offices for the time for which they were elected ,
for the Ninth District as it is changed under this Act ; provided ,
however , that if either of them is not on the effective date of
this Act a resident of Hardin county , he shall not be allowed
to retain his respective office , unless he shall remove his resi-
dence , within a reasonable time , into Hardin county .
Section 4. The office of circuit judge and Commonwealth's
attorney for the new judicial district as established under this
Act are hereby created . Until the regular election for circuit
judges and Commonwealth's attorneys , said offices shall be
filled as provided in Section 152 of the Constitution for offices
in which there is a vacancy .
Section 5. If any provision or portion of this Act , or the
application of such provisions or portions to any judicial district
affected hereby should be held invalid, the remainder of this
Act , and the application of such remaining provisions shall not
be affected thereby .
Section 6. Whereas , there is a considerable backlog of
cases pending before the Hardin circuit court and the circuit
courts of the counties of Grayson , Breckinridge , and Meade , an
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall become effec-
tive immediately upon
itspassage and approval
bythe Governor
.
Approved March 12
, 1956

entering upon the discharge of his duties , the director shall give a

such function . Such agreement shall be in writing by the other
party to the agreement , in the necessary form , and the acceptance
shall be in the form of a resolution of the fiscal court . When
such resolution becomes effective the exercise of the powers and
duties to which the agreement relates, including those provided
in KRS 98.010 , shall be transferred to the department and the
other party shall be exempt from all further responsibility for
the exercise of the powers and duties so transferred during the
life of the agreement .
( b ) Such agreement may be revoked at the option of either
party by a resolution or ordinance or order of the revoking party
filed in the office of the county clerk , and a copy shall be delivered
to the other party . Such revocation shall become effective at
the end of the fiscal year occurring at least one year following
the filing of said resolution or order . In the absence of such
revocation so filed, the agreement shall continue indefinitely .
( c ) The transfer of property and records of other state or
local public welfare activities shall be according to the terms of
the agreement .
Section 6. The fiscal court shall appoint a bipartisan
county welfare advisory board of not less than five nor more than
nine residents of the county . The members shall be appointed in
such a manner that the term of at least one member shall expire
annually . Any vacancy shall be filled for the unexpired term in the
same manner as the original appointment . Members of the
county welfare advisory board shall serve without compensation .
The county welfare advisory board shall suggest
rules and regulations to the county director of welfare with re-
spect to the organization of the county department of welfare ,
and shall advise the director and the fiscal court with respect
to budget estimates , policies and other problems of welfare
activities .

CHAPTER 4
(S.B. 5 )
Approved March 14, 1956
AN ACT relating to the operations of combined electric and water
systems in cities of the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Immediately upon the effective date of any
Act of the General Assembly changing the classification of any
city of the third class to a city of the second class, if such
city while operating as a city of the third class had accepted
and was operating under the provisions of KRS 96.171 to KRS
96.188 , inclusive , relating to the operation of a combined elec-
tric and water system , such city of the second class shall con-
tinue to operate such combined electric and water plant under
the provisions of said sections of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes as amended and said sections , and each of them , as amended ,
hereby are adopted by reference and shall apply to such city
of the second class and the combined electric and water sys-
tem of such city of the second class shall be operated under
the provisions of KRS 96.172 and 96.188 , as amended . Said
sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes shall govern the
appointment , qualifications , tenure , powers and duties of the
members of the electric and water plant board of the city and
the powers and duties of such board . The members of such
board shall hold office for the respective terms to which they

16 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
were appointed and qualified , and upon the expiration of their
respective terms and upon the occurrence of a vacancy , the
appointments to fill such expired terms or such vacancies shall
be made as provided in said statutes . Such appointments shall
be made by the mayor and approved by the legislative body of
the city. All contracts and obligations incurred by such board ,
and between the city and such board , shall remain in full force
and effect whether made after or prior to such city becoming a
city of the second class .

lists . If there are two or more contending executive committees
of the same party in the county , the committee that is recognized
by the state central committee , by written certificate of the chair-
man thereof , shall be recognized by the state board in making
the appointments . The county commissioners shall qualify by
taking , before the county clerk , an oath faithfully to perform their
duties according to law. The clerk shall make a certificate of
the oath and file it in his office. Vacancies shall be filled by ap-
pointment by the state board from the names already designated ,
and the person appointed to fill the vacancy shall be of the same
political party as his predecessor .

the exact amount to be fixed by the fiscal court and paid
monthly . In all other counties , each member of the county

fifteen dollars per day for each day in session , and , in addition ,
the secretary may receive compensation for fifteen days while

county boards of registration and purgation in counties other

AND EMPLOYEES GENERALLY
Section 1. Section 64.640 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Except as otherwise provided in subsection ( 2 ) of
this section , and excepting officers elected by popular vote, com-
missioners of the Court of Appeals , employees of the General
Assembly , including employees of the Legislative Research Com-
mission , members of boards and commissions , and presidents and
employees of the University of Kentucky and the state colleges ,
the Department of Personnel shall prepare a schedule of com-
pensation , payable out of the State Treasury , with a minimum
and maximum salary rate, and such intermediate salary rates as
are deemed necessary or advisable , for the office or position of
employment of every state officer and employee , including spe-
cifically the offices and positions of employment in every con-
stitutional administrative department , statutory administrative
department , independent agency , or other unit of state govern-
ment . Such schedules shall be based upon studies of the duties
and responsibilities of the offices and positions and upon a com-
parison with rates being paid for similar or comparable services
elsewhere , and in the preparation of such schedules , the De-
partment of Personnel shall ascertain and record the duties ,
responsibilities and authority pertaining to the various offices
and positions in the state service, and classify such positions in
the manner provided in Article IV of this Act. No such schedule
shall become effective until it has been approved by the Gov
ernor .

or custodial services at institutions where the character of the
work, the relatively low rate of compensation , or the place of
work make it impracticable to secure at stated times a sufficient
number of applicants to supply the needs of the service , the
department may provide by regulation for the testing of appli-
cants , singly or in groups , at stated places for such unskilled or
semiskilled labor , and at institutions or elsewhere for domestic ,
attending , housekeeping or custodial service, the tests to be
given at any time on due notice thereof but without public
announcement .

Section 10. Section 186.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows:

(1) "Department," as used in KRS 186.015 to 186.270, means the Department of Revenue. As used in KRS 186.400 to 186.640, it means the Department of Public Safety.

(2) "Highway" means every way or place of whatever nature when any part of it is open to the use of the public, as a matter of right, license or privilege, for the purpose of vehicular traffic.

(3) "Manufacturer" means any person engaged in manufacturing motor vehicles who will, under normal conditions during the year, manufacture or assemble at least ten new motor vehicles.

(4) "Motor vehicle," as used in KRS 186.020 to 186.260, includes all vehicles, as defined in paragraph (a) of subsection (7) of this section, which are propelled otherwise than by muscular power. As used in KRS 186.400 to 186.640, it means all vehicles, as defined in paragraph (b) of subsection (7) of this section, which are self-propelled.

(5) "Operator" means any person in actual control of a motor vehicle upon a highway.

(6) "Owner" means a person who holds the legal title of a vehicle. If a vehicle is the subject of an agreement for the conditional sale or lease, with the right of purchase upon performance of the conditions stated in the agreement and with an immediate right of possession vested in the conditional vendee or lessee, or if a mortgagor of a vehicle is entitled to possession, the conditional vendee or lessee or mortgagor shall be deemed the owner.

(7) (a) "Vehicle," as used in KRS 186.020 to 186.260, includes all agencies for the transportation of persons or property over or upon the public highways of this Commonwealth

ARTICLE XII . LEGISLATIVE RESEARCH COMMISSION
Section 1. Section 7.090 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1) There is hereby created a Legislative Research Com-
mission composed of the Lieutenant Governor , the President Pro
Tem of the Senate , the Speaker of the House of Representatives ,
and the majority and minority Floor Leaders of the Senate and
House of Representatives . Any vacancy in the commission shall
be filled by the remaining members thereof , provided , if the
vacancy is in the membership of the House of Representatives
then the successor shall be from the House , and if the vacancy
is from the Senate membership of the commission then the suc-
cessor shall be from the Senate . A member thus elected to fill
any vacancy shall hold office for the unexpired term of his pred-
The Lieutenant Governor shall be ex-officio chair-ecessor .
man of the commission .
( 2 ) The Legislative Research Commission shall meet dur-
ing regular and special sessions of the General Assembly , and
during the intervals between sessions at such times and places

CHAPTER 7 55
as the chairman may determine . Meetings of the commission shall
be called by the chairman on his own initiative , or at the written
request of any three members of the commission . For attending
meetings between sessions whether regular or special , of the Gen-
eral Assembly , the members of the commission , or any subcom-
mittee thereof , shall be paid their necessary traveling expenses
and in addition thereto twenty -five dollars per day.
(3 ) The commission shall appoint a director , who may be
a member of the commission , who shall have had graduate train-
ing in government in a recognized university or college or prac-
tical experience in governmental administration , and who shall
hold office at the pleasure of the commission . If a member of

Commission to carry on a program of study , review and re-
examination of the Constitution , continuing the program and
functions formerly performed by the Constitution Review Com-

determine to what extent the Constitution requires amendment
or revision ; to analyze and appraise suggestions for amendment
or revision advanced by interested persons or groups ; and to

Section 1. Section 164.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes

The terms of each member, except the holdover members ,
appointed under the provisions of this Act shall begin on April
1, 1957. As of April 1, 1957, the Governor shall appoint two
members to the Board of Regents for each of the four state
colleges , except Kentucky State College , and shall appoint three
members to the Board of Regents of Kentucky State College .
One of the members of the Board of Regents of each state
college , except Kentucky State College , shall be appointed
for a term of two years , and one for a term of four years .
As of April 1, 1957 , the Governor shall appoint three mem-
bers of the Board of Regents of Kentucky State College ,
one member for a term of two years , one member for a
term of three years , one member for a term of four
years .
Thereafter , appointments for a term of four years shall be
made by the Governor within thirty days after the expiration
of the term of each member . If the Governor should fail to
make any such appointment within thirty days after the expira-
tion of the term of any member , the remaining members of the
Board of Regents of the particular college , whose terms shall not

58 ACTS , FIRST EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
have expired , shall make such appointment within sixty days
after the expiration of such term .
Not more than two appointed members of any board shall
be residents of one county ; not more than three of the appointed
members of any board shall belong to the same political party .
Membership on the board shall not be incompatible with any
other state office .
Appointments to fill vacancies shall be made in the same
manner and within the same time after the occurrence of the
vacancy as regular appointments . The person appointed shall
hold the position for the unexpired term only .
Each appointed member of the board shall , at the first
meeting following his appointment , take an oath to faithfully
discharge his duties .
Each member of the board shall serve for the term for
which he is appointed and until his successor is appointed and
qualified .
Section 2. Subsection ( 2 ) of section 63.080 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes hereby is expressly made applicable to members
of the Board of Regents of each of said colleges .

Section 4. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is hereby created to read as follows :
The department shall have the power and duty :
( 1 ) To determine the comparative needs and demands
of the various state agencies for acquiring real estate and for
building projects .
( 2 ) To purchase , lease or rent such real estate as the de-
partment may find to be necessary for use by the state or by
any state agency, upon such terms and conditions and for such
consideration as may appear to be fair and reasonable.

state or by any state agency, found by the department not to
be needed for public use or to have become unsuitable for such
use , except that this power shall not extend to real estate as

Section 18. Section 41.220 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) Not less than three solvent banks shall be designated
as depositories for state funds . Each bank designated shall have
not less than the minimum capital stock as required in paragraphs
( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of KRS 287.070 . Banks shall be designated as de-
positories for state funds upon agreement of the State Treasurer
and the Commissioner of Finance . Those designated shall be
entered in the executive journal . If at any time it appears that
the capital of any depository has become impaired , the state's
deposits shall be withdrawn and another depository named .
(2 ) The State Treasurer and the Commissioner of Finance
shall determine the needs for moving state funds from one des-
ignated depository to another .

Kentucky's woodlands , directly reducing the benefits of healthy
forests to the people of the Commonwealth , and in the same in-
stance , wantonly remove millions of dollars each year from the
state's economy ; and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth is included in the southern forest area
where 85 out of every 100 forest fires occur ; and
WHEREAS , the loss of forest in the south amounts to 85 out of every

WHEREAS , the ratio of deliberately set fires in this region stands at
94 out of every 100, and the need for preventive measures in that
direction are legion ; and
WHEREAS , the forests of Kentucky are vital to water conservation ,
and the control of floods , contribute in general to the scenic beauty
of our state and furnish a habitat for wildlife , provide employment
to hundreds of our citizens , and are important from the standpoint
of wealth and industrial tax base ; and
WHEREAS , the Conference of Southern Governors has endorsed the
principle of forest wildfire prevention and the Southern Forest

PROCLAMATION
TO ALL TO WHOM THESE PRESENTS SHALL COME :
WHEREAS , the industrial development of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky is directly dependent upon an ade-
quate system of highway transportation ; and ,
WHEREAS , the present highway transportation system is, by
reasons of existing limitations , inadequate to the
needs of an expanding economy and is detrimental
to the location and expansion of industries in
the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and ,
WHEREAS , the system of highways in Kentucky is inadequate
to meet the demands of present day needs and
unsafe for modern travel ; and ,
WHEREAS , it is necessary to increase the revenues necessary
to the building of a modern system of highways ;
and ,
WHEREAS , it is essential that the Department of Highways
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky consult and
advise with the fiscal courts of the various coun-
ties on the subject of construction and improve-
ment of secondary and rural roads lying within
certain counties :

hereinabove set out , and in accordance with the

(S.B. 3 )
Approved March 28 , 1956
AN ACT authorizing the execution , sale and delivery of one hundred mil-
lion dollars ( $ 100,000,000 ) principal amount of Commonwealth of
Kentucky general obligation bonds , the proceeds from the sale of
which will be used to match federal funds for the construction of
highways , bridges and tunnels in the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and providing for the submission thereof to the voters at the general
election to be held on November 6, 1956.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Subject to the provisions of Sections 49 and 50
of the Constitution of Kentucky , there is hereby contracted on
behalf of the Commonwealth a debt in the amount of one hun-
dred million dollars ( $ 100,000,000 ) . It is hereby declared to be
the intent of the General Assembly in enacting this Act and
contracting the aforesaid debt on behalf of the Commonwealth
to provide a method whereby the Commonwealth of Kentucky
can issue bonds directly obligating the Commonwealth in an
amount of one hundred million dollars ( $ 100,000,000 ) for the
purpose of providing funds to match federal funds allocated to
Kentucky for the construction and reconstruction of highways ,
tunnels and bridges within the Commonwealth of Kentucky , sub

CHAPTER 3 85
ject to the approval of the people of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky as provided in Section 50 of the Constitution of Ken-
tucky ; such approval or disapproval to be expressed at the gen-
eral election to be held on November 6, 1956 .
Section 2. Bonds of the Commonwealth of Kentucky shall
be issued in the total amount of one hundred million dollars
( $ 100,000,000 ) . Said bonds shall be of the denomination of one
hundred dollars ( $ 100 ) , or such multiples thereof as the State
Property and Buildings Commission may determine ; shall be
engraved , lithographed or printed , as said commission shall de-
termine; shall be signed at the time of their respective issuance
by the Governor then in office, and at the same time attested by
the Secretary of State then in office, with the seal of the Com-
monwealth affixed . All of said bonds shall bear interest at such
rate, not exceeding three percent per annum , as said commis-
sion shall determine at the time of such issue . All of said bonds
shall bear date of January 1, 1957. The interest thereon shall
be payable semiannually January first and July first of each year
thereafter . There shall be attached to said bonds coupons for such
semiannual interest bearing the facsimile signature of the Gov-
ernor and the Treasurer of the Commonwealth , the last coupon
on each bond being due at the date of maturity of the bond to
which it is attached . Said bonds and coupons shall be payable in
lawful money of the United States of America at such place or
places as the State Property and Buildings Commission may de-
termine . Said bonds , at the request of the owners , may be regis-
tered as to principal and interest with the Department of Finance
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky upon the payment of a regis-
tration fee of fifty cents on each one thousand dollars principal
amount of said bonds . All of said bonds and the interest thereon
shall be exempt from state , county and municipal taxation . Save as
in this Act provided , the form of said bonds , and the method of
registration , shall be determined by the State Property and
Buildings Commission . The cost of engraving , lithographing or
printing said bonds shall be certified by the State Property and
Buildings Commission to the Department of Finance for pay-
ment and shall be paid out of the State Road Fund .
Section 3. Said bonds shall bear serial numbers beginning
with number one and shall mature on such dates, with such pro

86 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
visions as to prior redemption including premiums therefor , as
may be determined by the State Property and Buildings Com-
mission , providing that all of such bonds shall mature within
thirty years from January 1, 1957 .
Section 4. The bonds herein provided for shall be direct
obligations of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the full faith
and credit of the Commonwealth hereby is pledged for the pay-
ment of said bonds and the interest thereon . In order to pro-
vide for the payment of the principal of

Section 9. The proceeds derived from the sale of the afore-
said bonds shall be held or invested by the State Property and
Buildings Commission in the same manner and under the same re-
strictions set forth in section 8 hereof as to the sinking fund and
shall be used solely and only by the Department of Highways
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky to match federal funds
allocated to Kentucky for the construction and reconstruction
of highways , tunnels and bridges within the Commonwealth of
Kentucky.
Section 10. This Act shall be submitted to the people at the
general election to be held on November 6, 1956 , as provided in
Section 50 of the Constitution of Kentucky . It shall be the duty
of the Secretary of State to cause this Act to be published once a
week for four consecutive weeks next preceding the week of the
general election to be held on November 6, 1956 , in at least three
newspapers of general circulation published in the state , and the
Secretary of State shall also cause to be published at the same
time and in the same manner the fact that the indebtedness
authorized by this Act shall be submitted to the people for their
approval or rejection at the general election aforesaid . Each such
publication shall bear the title , "Notice of Election ." The Secre-
tary of State shall cause to be included at the foot of said publica-
tion the statement that the Act will be submitted to the voters
of the Commonwealth at said regular election to be held on
November 6, 1956. The form of the question to be placed upon
the ballot shall be : "Are you in favor of the Act of the General
Assembly known as Senate Bill No. 3, enacted by the Second
Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly of 1956 , authoriz-
ing the Commonwealth to issue and sell bonds of the principal
amount not exceeding one hundred million dollars ($ 100,000,000 ),
to provide funds to match federal aid in the construction and re-
construction of highways , bridges and tunnels in Kentucky ?" It
shall be the duty of the Secretary of State , not less than forty days
before the November election in 1956 , to certify to the county

22,000 pounds or less may have it registered as a farmer's truck
and obtain a license for $4.50 by filing with the county clerk , in

or dairy products , that he owns a truck of the gross weight of
22,000 pounds or less , that he has caused to be printed upon each

(5) Any person owning a truck or bus used solely in trans-

( d) A certificate of title , if last registered in another state
where the law of such state requires the owner of a trailer , semi-
trailer or house - trailer to obtain a certificate of title or owner-
ship; or
(e ) Affidavit from the owner of a trailer , semi-trailer or
house- trailer assembled or constructed for his personal use on
the highways ; or
(f) Affidavit from the owner of a trailer , semi -trailer or
house -trailer where the bill of sale for such vehicle has been
lost, destroyed or stolen .
The affidavit required in paragraph ( e ) shall contain the
owner's name , address , date , brief description and a statement
that the trailer was constructed by the owner for use on the
highways and any additional information that the department
may from time to time prescribe by regulation .
The affidavit required in paragraph ( f ) shall contain the
owner's name, address , date , make , year made , serial or identi-
fication number , from whom purchased , date of purchase , that
the person making the affidavit is the sole owner , the circum-
stances under which the bill of sale was lost , destroyed or stolen ,
and any additional information that the department may from
time to time prescribe by regulation .
After that , he shall register his trailer , semi -trailer or house-
trailer on or before March 1 of each year . Insofar as the owner
is concerned , registration with the clerk shall be deemed to be
registration with the department .
Section 3. A trailer , semi -trailer or house -trailer last regis

CHAPTER 6 99
tered in another state and purchased by a person for operation in
this state shall be first registered in this state by and in the name
of the purchaser and not in the name of the seller .
Section 4. If an owner because of sickness or absence from
the state is unable to renew the registration of his trailer , semi-
trailer or house - trailer on or before March 1, he will be deemed
to have complied with the law, if before operating the trailer ,
semi -trailer or house-trailer in this state he registers it at some
later date and pays the fee for the full year , just as though he
had renewed the registration on or before March 1. The owner
shall file with the application an affidavit showing sickness or
absence .
Section 5. The application blank shall contain blank spaces
for , and the applicant shall fill in the owner's name and post
office address , make of the trailer , semi -trailer or house - trailer ,
and serial or identification number . Every application shall be
accompanied by the appropriate fee .
Section 6. Upon filing application and paying the fee as
provided by law, said owner shall be given a receipt containing
the registration number and the information included in the
application and one plate for the registration year bearing said
number .
Section 7. The annual registration fee for trailers and semi-
trailers which are drawn by motor vehicles required to be licensed
under section 186.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes shall be
$ 4.50 . The provisions of this Act shall not apply to privately
owned and operated trailers used for the transportation of boats ,
luggage , personal effects , farm products , farm supplies or farm
equipment .
The registration fee for house-trailers shall be $9.50.
The clerk shall issue the registration plate furnished by the
department and shall be paid for this service the sum of fifty
cents .
Section 8. If any person becomes the owner of a trailer ,
semi -trailer or house -trailer after January 1 of any year , and for
that reason registers for only part of a year the registration fee

100 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
shall be as many twelfths of the annual fee as there are unex-
pired months in the calendar year .
Section 9. The department shall prescribe a plate of prac-
tical form and size which shall contain only the registration
number and the word "Trailer" and the abbreviation of the word
"Kentucky" .
Section 10. Sections 186.070 through 186.240 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes shall apply to the registration of trailers ,
semi -trailers and house -trailers in so far as practicable .
Section 11. Trailers , semi -trailers and house-trailers as de-
fined in this Act are expressly exempted from sections 138.450
through 138.470 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Section 11A . Trailers , semi-trailers and house-trailers in
transit between a manufacturing plant and dealer's place of
business ; between a manufacturing plant and user's place of
residence ; or between a dealer's place of business and user's place
of residence and farm equipment pulled or towed by a motor
vehicle not required to be licensed under the provisions of Chap-
ter 186 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes shall be exempt from
the provisions of this Act .
Section 12. The Department of Revenue shall administer
the provisions of this Act .

permit , and such limitation or restriction shall remain in effect .

such a false or fraudulent statement or claim for refund , shall
be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction thereof shall
be fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more than five
hundred dollars , or imprisoned not exceeding one year , or both ,
in the discretion of the court .
(19 ) Any person violating any provision of KRS 138.630
is guilty of a misdemeanor , unless the act is by any other law
of this state declared to be a felony , and upon conviction is
punishable by a fine of not less than $19 nor more than $500,
or by imprisonment in the county jail for not less than thirty
days nor more than six months , or by both such fine and im-
prisonment . The fine and imprisonment provided for in this
subsection shall be in addition to any other penalty imposed by
any other provision of KRS 138.560 to 138.640 .
(20) Any person violating any provision of KRS 138.655
to KRS 138.725 is guilty of a misdemeanor and upon conviction
is punishable by a fine of not less than $19 nor more than $500
or by imprisonment in the county jail for not less than thirty
days nor more than six months or by both such fine and
imprisonment .
(21 ) Any person found operating any motor vehicle in
this state requiring a license under the provisions of KRS 138.560
thru 138.640 or 138.655 thru 138.725 without such license or
after such license has been surrendered , cancelled , or revoked
may , in addition to the penalties provided in subsections ( 19 )
and ( 20 ) of this section have such motor vehicle seized by any
officer duly authorized to enforce the provisions of this Act .

CHAPTER 10 137
(22 ) The fine and imprisonment provided for in sub-
section ( 20 ) of this section shall be in addition to any other
penalty imposed by any other provisions of KRS 138.640 to
KRS 138.725 .
Section 29. Sections 138.605 , 138.610 , 138.650 and 138.700
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes and section 1, subsection ( 9 ) ,
of House Bill 479 passed by the 1956 Regular Session of the
Kentucky General Assembly are hereby repealed.
Section 30. Whereas , it is to the best interest of the people
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky that the provisions of this
Act take effect at the earliest possible date , an emergency is de-
clared to exist and this Act shall become effective immediately
upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
.

PROCLAMATION
TO ALL TO WHOM THESE PRESENTS SHALL COME :
WHEREAS , the budgetary needs of the government have not
yet been fully determined by the General Assem-
bly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS , such budgetary needs must be determined before
the amount of revenue to meet such needs can be
ascertained;
NOW, THEREFORE , in order to determine the budgetary
needs of government , and according to the power
vested in me by the Constitution of Kentucky to
convene the General Assembly into Extraordinary
Session, I do hereby issue this , my proclamation ,
convening the General Assembly of Kentucky in
Extraordinary Session at the seat of government
at Frankfort , Kentucky at 11:00 o'clock AM, on
Thursday , March 29 , 1956 , for the sole purpose
of considering the following subject :
( 1 ) To make appropriations for the support of
government and thereby fix the budgetary require-
ments of the government of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky .
You will therefore , in accordance with this proclamation ,
assemble in Extraordinary Session at the seat of government
at Frankfort , Kentucky , on the day and date above written , to
consider the subject herein mentioned , and only said subject .
Done at Frankfort , Kentucky , this 28th day of
March in the year of our Lord , one thousand nine
hundred and fifty -six, and in the year of the Com-
monwealth , the one hundred and sixty -fourth .
( Signed ) A. B. CHANDLER , Governor
( Signed ) Thelma L. Stovall
Secretary of State

budgeted for teachers ' salaries is not equal to or greater than
the amount to be included in the foundation program for a
district for teachers ' salaries as determined under Kentucky Re-
vised Statute 157.390 . No working budget or salary schedule
shall be approved in which the average salary paid any rank of
teacher is not at least equivalent to the allotment for that rank

CHAPTER 1 157
and in which any teacher is paid less than ninety percent of the
foundation program allotment for that rank .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot to
any school district more basic classroom units than the number
of teachers employed by that school district for basic classroom
units . No basic classroom units shall be allotted which increase
in 1956-57 or 1957-58 the differential between the pupil-teacher
ratio of the secondary grades ( 9-12 ) and the elementary grades
( 1-8 ) in 1955-56 . No basic classroom units shall be approved
which provide in a given building or school a lower pupil-teacher
ratio than twenty -seven in average daily attendance to one , or
the pupil - teacher ratio which obtained in 1955-56 , which ever
is lower .
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall allot class-
room units for superintendents , principals , their certified assis-
tants and special instructional personnel as provided in Kentucky
Revised Statute 157.360 , subsection ( 6 ) , only for personnel who
are qualified for their respective positions under provisions of
law and regulations of the State Board of Education and who
maintain programs approved under regulations of the State
Board of Education . The State Board of Education may desig-
nate ASIS units which may be allotted either as basic units or
ASIS units , but otherwise no excess of ASIS units shall be used
to compensate for a deficit in basic units .
The number of units allotted by the Superintendent of
Public Instruction for vocational education and the education
of exceptional children shall not exceed the following :
1956-57 1957-58
Vocational Agriculture 259 274
Vocational Home Economics 351 381
Trades and Industries 65 75
Distributive Occupations 13 17
Exceptional Children 120 135
The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall not allot a
full classroom unit for any one-teacher school with fewer than 25
pupils in average daily attendance unless it has been determined
on the basis of a survey that no facilities are available to which
these pupils could be transported under regulations of the State

158 ACTS , THIRD EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
Board of Education and no full classroom units shall be allotted
for any one teacher school which has fewer than 12 pupils in
average daily attendance .
Facilities that are used to house school children shall be
approved by the Superintendent of Public Instruction under
Kentucky Revised Statute 162.060 and 162.160 . No alterations
or additions of old buildings shall be made without prior approval
of the Superintendent of Public Instruction . No space shall be
rented or purchased and converted to classroom use without
prior approval of the Superintendent of Public Instruction . The
State Board of Education may adopt regulations for the approval
on an annual basis of temporary facilities for the housing of
school children but these regulations shall not permit the division
of any present room by partitions of non -structural material . No
classroom units shall be allotted for a second teacher in the
same classroom . No facilities shall be approved for classroom
use which are detrimental to the health of or dangerous to the
safety of school children .
If a school district operates any school or grade of a school
for a term of less than nine scholastic months or for a school day
of less than the minimum required by Kentucky Revised Statute
158.060 , the average daily attendance of such school shall be
reduced proportionately by the Superintendent of Public In-
struction in determining the allotment of classroom units pro-
vided for under Kentucky Revised Statute 157.360 , subsection
( 2 ) except as provided for by regulations of the State Board of
Education .
If at the time of the tentative allotment of school funds
under Kentucky Revised Statutes 157.310-157.440 it shall be
determined that insufficient revenue is available to finance in
full the foundation program including a provision that no dis-
trict receive less than $ 80 per child in average daily attendance
and no district receive less total state aid than it received from
the Per Capita Fund in 1955-56 , the Superintendent of Public
Instruction shall not allot any classroom units under Kentucky
Revised Statute 157.360 , subsection ( 3 ) .

PART FOUR
GENERAL PROVISION
1. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1956 and
nothing in this Act is to be construed to amend or alter the
provisions of Chapter 42 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the duties and powers of the Commissioner of Finance .
All questions that arise in interpreting any appropriation in this
Act as to the purpose for which the same may be expended shall
be decided by the Commissioner of Finance in accordance with
the detailed estimates embodied in the budget report as con-
sidered by the General Assembly , and the decision of the Com-
missioner of Finance shall be final and conclusive .
2. Whereas , the finances of the Commonwealth can not be

180 ACTS , THIRD EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
ascertained at this time , and since the actual needs of each of-
ficer , department , board , commission , institution , or subdivision
of the state government can only be anticipated and not finally
determined by the legislature , and since the total amount of
revenue accruing to the General Expenditure Fund from which
these appropriations are hereby made can only be anticipated
through existing laws and those that may hereafter be enacted ,
it is the purpose and intention of this Act that the expenditures
for each fiscal year under Part I in this Act shall not exceed the
total revenues anticipated to accrue to the General Expenditure
Fund for the same fiscal year . As the conditions can not be
finally determined until after the adjournment of the 1956
General Assembly , and as the needs of each officer , department ,
board , commission , institution or subdivision of the state govern-
ment and the total revenues accruing to the General Fund can
not be fully determined at this time , it is the intention and the
purpose of this General Assembly that the Commissioner of
Finance , with the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and
the approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth , be and
he is hereby authorized , empowered and directed to ascertain
and determine the needs of each officer , department , board ,
commission , institution or subdivision of the state government ,
and to ascertain the total revenue anticipated to accrue to the
General Expenditure Fund of the Commonwealth , and upon the
ascertainment and determination of said facts , he , the Commis-
sioner of Finance , in the manner set forth and as provided by
general law, is hereby authorized , empowered and directed by
the General Assembly to prevent an overdraft or deficit in any
fiscal year for which appropriations are herein made , by equi-
tably reducing , according to need, without discrimination , the
appropriations herein made in Part I to any officer , department ,
board , commission , institution , or subdivision of the state gov-
ernment ; provided that the power hereby vested shall not permit
any reduction of the appropriation of any officer , department ,
board , commission , institution or subdivision of the state govern-
ment that will actually impair the necessary constitutional
functions of any agency whose operations and functions are de-
termined to be necessary constitutional functions of government .
3. In enacting this Budget Appropriation Law , it is the
deliberate intention of the General Assembly to enact each sec

CHAPTER 2 181
tion , and each subsection thereof , as a separate and/or specific
appropriation and law, and if any section or any subsection there-
of shall be held invalid, or unconstitutional the decision of the
courts shall not affect or impair any of the remaining sections ,
subsections , or provisions contained herein .
4. All continuing appropriations in existence at the time
this Act takes effect are hereby discontinued and repealed by
this Act , except as provided by Chapters 12, 42, 45, and 56 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes . All laws and parts of laws in
conflict with any of the provisions of the above enactments , to
the extent of such conflict , are hereby repealed .

A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION appropriating money to the Depart-
ment of Finance for restoring the executive mansion and repairing
and replacing the furnishings in the executive mansion .
WHEREAS , the furniture and furnishings within the executive mansion
are in a state of disrepair , and it is necessary to replace many items ;
and
WHEREAS , the executive mansion is the property of the people of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky ; provided by them for the residence of
their elected chief executive officer ; and
WHEREAS , the people of the Commonwealth of Kentucky expect the
executive and legislative departments of state government to take
necessary action to preserve their investment in the executive man-
sion , as well as other property owned by the state and its people,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
There is hereby appropriated to the Department of Finance
for the 1956-57 fiscal year the sum of twenty five thousand
dollars ( $25,000 ) to be used for restoring the Executive Mansion
and for repairing and replacing furniture and furnishings in the
Executive Mansion .
Approved April 11 , 1956

182 ACTS , THIRD EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
CHAPTER 3
(S.R. 6)
A CONCURRENT RESOLUTION appropriating money to Elections-
For County Expenses ( state share ) , and declaring and emergency .
WHEREAS , there is not sufficient money appropriated to the account
for the state's share of county election expenses due to the change
in the date of the primary election ; and
WHEREAS , the counties do not have funds available to pay election ex-
penses due to the change in the primary election date ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. There is hereby appropriated to the Elections-
County Expenses ( state share ) account the sum of $165,000.00
to be used in reimbursing the county treasury of each county
for a portion of the expense of conducting the primary election
to be held May 29, 1956. The county treasurer in each county
shall certify to the Department of Finance the number of pre-
cincts in his county . Upon proper certification by the county
treasurer of the number of precincts in his county the Depart-
ment of Finance shall advance to each county treasurer the sum
of forty dollars per precinct .
Section 2. Whereas , there is an immediate need for money
to be made available in order that it can be advanced to county
treasurers for primary election expenses , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Resolution shall take effect upon
its
passage and approval by the Governor
.
Approved April
11, 1956

CHAPTER 1
(S. B. 2)
AN ACT relating to annexation of unincorporated territory by munici-
palities in Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) The General Assembly , recognizing that the general
welfare and prosperity of the Commonwealth of Kentucky is very
greatly dependent upon continued industrial development and
expansion; and , further recognizing that reasonable assurances
of fair treatment will greatly increase industrial development and
expansion in Kentucky , it hereby is declared to be the public
policy of the Commonwealth of Kentucky to encourage the loca-
tion of new industries and the expansion of existing industries in
Kentucky by prohibiting unfair and unreasonable annexation by
municipal corporations of industries now or hereafter located
in unincorporated areas . However , it is not the intent of the Gen-
eral Assembly to prohibit , restrict or hamper normal expansion of
municipal boundaries if such normal development and expansion
extends to and embraces such industrial properties .
(2 ) No unincorporated territory in which is located an
industrial plant or plants shall be annexed by any municipality un-
less such territory is embraced within a broad , comprehensive
plan of annexation . The territory to be annexed shall be contig-
uous to the boundary line or lines of such municipality , and the
territory or area to be annexed shall be both compact and con-
tiguous . The number of registered voters duly qualified to vote
in the territory proposed to be annexed shall equal or exceed
fifty ( 50 ) percent of the average number of persons employed
by industrial plants within such territory during the next pre-
ceding calendar year , the number of such registered voters

188 ACTS , FOURTH EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
to be determined by taking the total of such voters from the last
closed registration books in the county clerk's office .
( 3 ) Nothing herein shall be construed as prohibiting any
municipality from annexing any industrial plant or plants or
its
properties

ofsuch industrial

orplants consent
to
orrequest such action

asnow provided by existing

oflaws

CHAPTER 3
(S. B. 7)
AN ACT relating to unemployment insurance .
Approved May 2 , 1956
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 341.350 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Conditions of qualification for benefits . An unemployed
worker shall , except as provided in KRS 341.360 and 341.370 ,
be eligible for benefits with respect to any week of unemploy-
ment only if:
(1) He has made a claim for benefits ;
(2) He has registered for work with respect to such week
in accordance with regulations prescribed by the commission ;
(3) He is physically and mentally able to work ;
(4) He is available for suitable work , and is actively seek-
ing such work ;
(5) His base -period wages are not less than four hundred
and fifty dollars ; and
(6) He has served a waiting period of one week of un-
employment within his benefit year . No week of unemployment
shall be counted as a waiting period unless the worker has satis-
fied the conditions of subsections (1), (2), (3), (4) and (5) of this
section .
(7) The foregoing eligibility requirements and the con-
ditions of benefit disqualifications imposed by KRS 341.370 shall
be strictly construed .

Section 2. Section 141.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) With respect to taxable years beginning after December
31 , 1955 , an annual tax, consisting of a normal tax and a surtax ,
shall be paid for each taxable year by every resident individual
of this state , upon his entire net income as defined in this chapter .

CHAPTER 4 195
Normal tax shall be determined by applying the rates in sub-
section (2) to net income and subtracting allowable tax credits
provided in subsection (3) . Surtax shall be determined according
to the rates in subsection (4). The annual tax due shall be the
sum of the normal tax and the surtax .
(2) The following rates shall be applied to net income :
(a) Two percent of the amount of net income not exceeding
three thousand dollars ;
(b) Three percent of the amount of net income in excess
of three thousand dollars but not in excess of four thousand
dollars ;
(c) Four percent of the amount of net income in excess of
four thousand dollars but not in excess of five thousand dollars ;
(d) Five percent of the amount of net income in excess of
five thousand dollars but not in excess of eight thousand dollars ;
(e) Six percent of the amount of net income in excess of
eight thousand dollars .
(3) The following tax credits , when applicable , shall be
deducted from the result obtained under subsection (2) to arrive
at normal tax:
(a) Twelve dollars for an unmarried individual .
(b) Twelve dollars for a married individual filing a separate
return and an additional twelve dollars for the spouse of taxpayer
if a separate return is made by the taxpayer and if the spouse ,
for the calendar year in which the taxable year of the taxpayer
begins , has no gross income and is not the dependent of another
taxpayer; or twenty -four dollars for married persons filing a joint
return , provided neither spouse is the dependent of another
taxpayer . The determination of marital status for the purpose of
this section shall be made in the manner prescribed in Section
153 of the Internal Revenue Code .
(c) Twelve dollars credit for each dependent whose gross
income for the calendar year in which the taxable year of the
taxpayer begins is less than six hundred dollars , or who is a
child, as defined in Section 151 (e) (3) of the Internal Revenue
Code , of the taxpayer and who (i) has not attained the age of 19
at the close of the calendar year in which the taxable year of the
taxpayer begins , or (ii) is a student . No exemption shall be
al-
lowed for any dependent who has made
ajoint return with his
spouse
.

196 ACTS , FOURTH EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
(d) An additional twelve dollars credit if the taxpayer has
attained the age of sixty-five before the close of the taxable year .
(e) An additional twelve dollars credit for taxpayer's spouse
if a separate return is made by the taxpayer and if the taxpayer's
spouse has attained the age of sixty-five before the close of the
taxable year , and , for the calendar year in which the taxable
year of the taxpayer begins , has no gross income and is not the
dependent of another taxpayer .
(f) An additional twelve dollars credit if the taxpayer is
blind at the close of the taxable year .
(g) An additional twelve dollars credit for taxpayer's spouse
if a separate return is made by the taxpayer and if the taxpayer's
spouse is blind and, for the calendar year in which the taxable
year of the taxpayer begins , has no gross income and is not the
dependent of another taxpayer .
(h) In the case of nonresidents , the tax credits allowable
under this subsection shall be that proportion of those permitted
therein that is represented by the ratio of the taxpayer's net
income in this state to his total net income from all sources ; or,
if the taxpayer elects to pay the tax under KRS 141.025 , as rep-
resented by the ratio of the taxpayer's adjusted gross income in
this state to his total adjusted gross income .
(i) In the case of a fiduciary, other than an estate , the
allowable tax credit shall be two dollars .
(j) In the case of an estate , the allowable tax credit shall
be twelve dollars .
(4) The following rates shall be applied to the normal tax to
determine the surtax :
(a) Ten percent of the amount of normal tax not in excess
of twenty -five dollars ;
(b) Twenty percent of the amount of normal tax in excess
of twenty -five dollars but not in excess of one hundred dollars ;
(c) Thirty percent of the amount of normal tax in excess of
one hundred dollars .
(5) An annual tax shall be paid for each taxable year as
specified in this section upon the entire net income , except as
herein provided , from
alltangible property located
inthis state
,
from
allintangible property that has acquired
abusiness situs
inthis state
, and from business
, trade
, profession
, occupation
or
other activities carried on
inthis state
,by natural persons not

CHAPTER 4 197
residents of this state. A nonresident individual shall be taxable
only upon the amount of income received by him from labor
performed , business done or from other activities in this state ,
from tangible property located in this state , and from intangible
property that has acquired a business situs within this state . The
remainder of the income received by him shall be deemed non-
taxable by this state .
(6) Subject to the provisions of KRS 141.081 and KRS
141.082 , any individual may elect to pay the annual tax imposed
by KRS 141.025 in lieu of the tax levied under this section .

Section 4. Section 141.040 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Every corporation organized under the laws of this state
and every foreign corporation doing business in this state, or
deriving income from activities or other sources in this state ,
except state and national banks and trust companies , building
and loan associations organized under the laws of this state and
under the laws of the United States and making loans to members

only, insurance companies , including farmers' or other mutual
hail, cyclone , windstorm or fire insurance companies , insurers
and reciprocal underwriters , and religious , educational , charitable
and like corporations not organized or conducted for pecuniary
profit, shall pay for each taxable year a tax to be computed by
the department upon the taxable net income of the corporation .

seven percent of all in excess of twenty -five thousand dollars of
the taxable net income of the corporation determined as provided
in this chapter .

Section 7. Section 141.140 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1) If the taxpayer makes , or is required to make, a federal
income tax return , his income shall be computed for the purposes
of this chapter on the basis of the same calendar or fiscal year
required by the federal government , and he shall employ the
same methods of accounting required for federal income tax
purposes .
(2) If a return is made by an individual for a period of less
than one year , the net income , computed on the basis of the
period for which a separate return is made , shall be placed on an
annual basis by multiplying the amount thereof by the number
of days in the year and dividing by the number of days included
in the period for which the separate return is made . The tax

CHAPTER 4 203
payable shall be such part of the tax computed on the annual
basis as the number of days in the period is of the number of
days in the year .
(3) If a return is made by a corporation for a period of less
than one year , the taxable net income , computed on the basis of
the period for which a separate return is made, shall be placed
on an annual basis by multiplying the amount thereof by the
number of days in the year and dividing by the number of days
included in the period for which the separate return is made .
The tax payable shall be such part of the tax computed on the
annual basis as the number of days in the period is of the number
of days in the year .

Section 9. Section 141.180 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Every individual having for the taxable year a gross
income of six hundred dollars or more shall make to the Depart-
ment of Revenue a return stating specifically the items which
he claims as deductions and tax credits allowed by this chapter ,
except that if any individual who has attained the age of sixty-
five before the close of his taxable year shall be required to
make a return only if he has for the taxable year a gross income
of twelve hundred dollars or more . If the individual is unable
to make his own return , the return shall be made by a duly
authorized agent .
(2) An optional return properly filed under the provisions
of KRS 141.025 shall be deemed sufficient to satisfy the pro-
visions of this section .
(3) Each individual return shall be verified by a written
declaration that it is made under the penalties of perjury .
Section 10. Section 141.210 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) As soon as practicable after each return is received , the
department shall examine and audit it. If the amount of tax
computed by the department is greater than the amount re-
turned by the taxpayer, the excess shall be assessed by the de-
partment within four years from the date the return was filed,
except as provided in subsection (2) of this section , and except
that in the case of a failure to file a return or of a fraudulent

CHAPTER 4 205
return the excess may be assessed at any time and in the case
of a return wherein the taxpayer omits from net income an
amount properly includible therein which is in excess of twenty-
five percent of the amount of net income stated in the return
the excess may be assessed at any time within six years after
the return was filed; provided , however , that if any excess is
assessed on account of any income which has been returned for
taxation by any other taxpayer , the department , with the con-
sent of such other taxpayer , his personal representatives or heirs ,
shall reduce the amount of the excess assessed for each year
by the amount of the income tax paid for that year by such
other taxpayer on account of the income in question . A notice
of such assessment shall be mailed to the taxpayer . The time
herein provided may be extended by agreement between the
taxpayer and the department .
(2) For the purpose of subsection ( 1 ) of this section a return
filed before the last day prescribed by law for the filing thereof
shall be considered as filed on such last day .
(3) It shall be the duty of every taxpayer to notify the de-
partment in writing of every examination of his books and
records with respect to his net income as reported on his federal
income tax return within thirty days after he has or should have
had knowledge of the beginning of such examination by the
Internal Revenue Service . When any taxpayer executes a
waiver of the statute of limitations on deficiencies or over- as-
sessment of federal income taxes , he shall notify the department
in writing within thirty days from the date he signs such waiver .
Failure on the part of a taxpayer to notify the department with-
in the time prescribed of either of the above actions shall auto-
matically suspend the limitations set forth in subsection (1)
above , until ninety days after the department receives know-
ledge of such action .
Section 11. Section 141.300 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Every individual shall , at the time prescribed in sub-
section (3) of this section , make a declaration of his estimated
tax for the taxable year if his gross income from sources other
than wages upon which Kentucky income tax will be withheld
can reasonably be expected to exceed one hundred dollars for

206 ACTS , FOURTH EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
the taxable year and his gross income to be six hundred dollars
or more .
(2) In the declaration required under subsection (1) the
individual shall state :
(a) The amount which he estimates as the amount of tax
under KRS 141.020 for the taxable year ;
(b) The amount which he estimates as the credits for the
taxable year under KRS 141.310 and KRS 141.315 ;
(c) The excess of the amount estimated under subpara-
graph (a) over the amount estimated under subparagraph (b) ,
which excess for purposes of this chapter shall be considered the
estimated tax for the taxable year ;
(d) Such other information as may be prescribed in regula-
tions promulgated by the department .
(3) The declaration required under subsection ( 1 ) of this
section shall be filed with the department on or before April 15
of the taxable year , except that if the requirements of subsec-
tion (1 ) of this section are first met ,
(a) After April 1 and before July 2 of the taxable year , the
declaration shall be filed on or before July 15 of the taxable
year ; or
(b) After July 1 and before November 2 of the taxable
year , the declaration shall be filed on or before November 15
of the taxable year ; or
(c) After November 1 of the taxable year , the declaration
shall be filed on or before February 15 of the succeeding tax-
able year .
(4) An individual may make amendments of a declaration
filed during the taxable year under subsection (3) of this section ,
under regulations prescribed by the department .
(5) If on or before February 15 of the succeeding taxable
year the taxpayer files a return for the taxable year for which
the declaration is required and pays in full the amount com-
puted on the return as payable then , under regulations pre-
scribed by the department ,
(a) If the declaration is not required to be filed during
the taxable year , but is required to be filed on or before such
February 15, such return shall , for the purposes of this section ,
be considered as such declaration ; and

CHAPTER 4 207
(b) If the tax shown on the return , reduced by the credits
under KRS 141.350 , is greater than the estimated tax shown in
a declaration previously made or, in the last amendment thereof ,
such return shall , for the purposes of this section , be considered
as the amendment of the declaration permitted by subsection
(4) of this section to be filed on or before such February 15 .
(6) The department shall promulgate regulations governing
reasonable extensions of time for filing declarations and paying
the estimated tax. Except in the case of taxpayers who are
abroad , no such extension shall be for more than six months .
(7) If the taxpayer is unable to make his own declaration ,
the declaration shall be made by a duly authorized agent or by
the guardian or other person charged with the care of the
person or property of such taxpayer .
(8) For the purposes of KRS 131.190 , a declaration of
estimated tax shall be held and considered a return of income
under this chapter .
Section 12. Section 141.310 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Every employer making payment of wages on or after
July 1, 1956 shall deduct and withhold upon such wages a tax
determined in accordance with the tables set forth in KRS
141.360 or KRS 141.315 , as the case may be .
(2) If wages are paid with respect to a period which is not
a payroll period , the amount to be deducted and withheld shall
be that applicable in the case of a miscellaneous payroll period
containing a number of days , including Sundays and holidays ,
equal to the number of days in the period with respect to which
such wages are paid .

CHAPTER 7
( Н. В. 9 )
AN ACT relating to alcoholic beverages prohibition . This Act should be
known as the Warinner -Bates Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Title to contraband included in subsections ( 3 ) ,
(4) and ( 5) of KRS 244.180 seized in a dry territory shall be
vested in the Alcoholic Beverage Control Board , irrespective of
whether such contraband was seized by peace officers of the city
or county or state administrators or field representatives of the
department .
Section 2. The Alcoholic Beverage Control Board shall
cause a sale of such contraband as is included in section 1 of
this Act to be made and shall , after deducting the expense of

CHAPTER 8 227
transporting and selling such contraband , return one -half the
proceeds of such sale to the proper authorities of the city or
county in which such seizure was accomplished solely by the
peace officers of the city or county where such seizure occurred .
The remaining one-half shall be paid to the State Treasury and
shall be credited to the General Fund . Where the evidence for
the seizure of such contraband is obtained by the state adminis-
trators or field representatives of the department , the Alcoholic
Beverage Control Board shall cause a sale of such contraband
to be made and shall pay
allthe receipts
tothe State Treasury
and be credited to the General Fund
.

governments often realize
nogain from the sale

beverage contraband seized
indry territory

; and

anemer-
gency

toexist and this Act shall become effective
upon
itspassage and approval

AN ACT relating to the construction of bridges over boundary line
streams between this state and another and the reciprocal exemption
thereof from taxes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 132.205 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :

CHAPTER 10 229
(1) Any bridge built by an adjoining state, by the govern-
ment of the United States , or by any commission created by an
act of Congress , over a boundary line stream between this state
and an adjoining state , if not operated for a profit, and if
connecting with a primary highway of this state, is declared
to be public property used for public purposes and exempt from
taxation unless the adjoining state, or other public body con-
structing such bridge , taxes similar bridges built by this Com-
monwealth in like manner .
(2) The issuance of bonds for the purpose of amortizing
the cost of construction of such bridges as is described in sub-
section (1) hereof shall not affect the tax exemption granted
herein .

AN ACT relating to a tax on amusement admission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 138.020 is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 4) and ( 5 ) of this
section, and in KRS 97.130 , an excise tax shall be paid on the
sale of admission to places of amusement or entertainment at
the following rates : Admission charges of fifty cents or less shall
not be subject to tax; if the admission charge is in excess of fifty
cents and does not exceed fifty-eight cents, the rate shall be five
cents ; if the admission charge is in excess of fifty- eight cents and
does not exceed one dollar, the rate shall be five cents plus one
cent additional for each ten cents or fractional part thereof in
excess of fifty-eight cents ; if the admission charge is in excess
of one dollar , the rate shall be ten cents plus one cent additional
for each twenty -five cents or fractional part thereof in excess
of one dollar .
(2 ) In the case of places of amusement or entertainment
at which food or drink is served and professional entertainers
are employed , the charge for admission shall be deemed to be
twenty - five percent of the total charge made or the amount of
the cover charge, if any, whichever is greater .
(3 ) In the case of all amusements or entertainments , the

230 ACTS , FOURTH EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
charge for admission shall be deemed to be the total charge
imposed , and if there is a charge for seating or for special
facilities within the place of amusement or entertainment , the
charge for admission shall be deemed to include both the charge
for general admission and the charge for the seating or other
special facilities provided .
(4) Nothing in this section shall be construed to impose
a tax upon admissions to dramatic or musical productions pre-
sented by a civic organization in a park owned by any municipal
corporation , if no pecuniary profits result to any member of the
organization , and if such pecuniary profits as may result to the
organization are used exclusively for civic, municipal , educa-
tional , charitable or religious purposes . No admissions shall be
tax -free under this subsection unless , prior to the sale thereof ,
application for exemption is made to the Department of Revenue
on forms and in accordance with rules and regulations duly
promulgated by it, and unless the department , after being satis-
fied that such admissions are tax-free, and in accordance with
such rules and regulations , issues a certificate of exemption .
( 5 ) ( a ) Nothing in this section shall be construed to im-
pose a tax upon admissions to athletic contests , plays , concerts
or other forms of amusements or entertainment presented by
nonprofit civic , fraternal , charitable , musical , social or educa-
tional clubs or associations if no professional performers are
employed and

CHAPTER 11 231
CHAPTER 11
(H. R. 4)
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the Legislative
Session Fund and declaring an emergency .
WHEREAS , the funds appropriated to the Legislative Session Fund for
the present biennium are almost exhausted, and
WHEREAS , present funds may not be adequate to pay members and
employees of the General Assembly for the duration of the extra-
ordinary sessions of the General Assembly ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That there is appropriated to the Legislative Ses-
sion Fund out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State
Treasury the sum of one hundred thousand dollars for the 1955-56
fiscal year .
Section 2. Whereas , funds previously appropriated to the
General Assembly are insufficient to meet the expenses of the
extraordinary sessions , an emergency is declared to exist , and this
Resolution shall become effective upon
itspassage and approval
by the Governor
.
Approved May
2, 1956

CHAPTER II
EXTRAORDINARY SESSION

CHAPTER II
EXTRAORDINARY SESSION

(a) A charge for seating or for special
admission to the legislative
chambers shall be deemed to include both the charge
for the present biennium and the charge
for the extra-ordinary session of the General Assembly.

WHEREAS, the present funds are not adequate to pay members
and employees of the General Assembly for the duration of the extra-
ordinary session of the General Assembly;

THEREFORE, NOW
BE IT RESOLVED by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky:

Section 1. That there is appropriated to the Legislative Ses-
sion Fund out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State
Treasury the sum of one hundred thousand dollars for the 1925-26
fiscal year.

Section 2. Whereas funds previously appropriated to the
General Assembly are insufficient to meet the expenses of the
extraordinary sessions an emergency is declared to exist and this
Resolution shall become effective upon its passage and approval
by the Governor.

Approved May 2, 1926

(b) If any professional entertainers or performers are
employed, all amounts paid for admission are subject to tax
unless at least seventy-five percent of the gross proceeds of
admissions are used for charitable, religious or educational pur-
poses within this state.

(c) It is the intent of this section to impose the tax once
only upon sales of the same services.

Approved April 27, 1926
